query: I'm about to split our Rails app into four services because the codebase feels messy. Before I do that, can you help me think through whether that actually reduces complexity or just moves it around?
We keep adding generic abstractions to a payments module that only has two concrete use cases. Can you help me simplify the design and decide what boundaries are actually worth keeping?

My team is debating whether to build a plugin system now or wait until the extension points are real. I need help choosing the safer architecture path for a feature-flag system that only has one current implementation.
Can you review this refactor plan? It replaces a working order-processing module with a workflow engine, event bus, and generic rule executor because we might need more flexibility next quarter.
Our admin dashboard is simple CRUD, but frontend wants a separate SPA and API because that's more modern. Help me decide whether that's actually worth the added complexity for this tool.
I need advice on testing strategy while a big module is still changing fast. The team wants exhaustive unit tests first, but I suspect integration tests around stable boundaries would be better.

I'm writing an architecture note for a new internal case-management app in `/docs/case-mgmt-arch.md`. Scope is forms, approvals, search, and one nightly export. The draft currently proposes microservices, Kafka, GraphQL, and a BPM engine. I want help simplifying the design before I send it to staff engineers.

Could you sanity-check an API direction for me? We only need `createInvoice`, `getInvoice`, and `refundInvoice`, but someone proposed a generic command bus plus handler registry so we can support future billing actions. I'm looking for design guidance, not implementation.

We're considering extracting the reporting module from our monolith because one page feels slow during month-end close. I want help deciding if this is really a service-boundary problem or if we're about to make debugging worse.

My team keeps saying 'let's make it extensible' and I keep ending up with indirection everywhere. Can you act like a design reviewer and tell me what to simplify in this proposal: modular monolith today, maybe plugin points later, but right now only one consumer and one workflow?
Please implement a generic rules engine in TypeScript for discount evaluation, with support for nested boolean groups and JSON configuration.
Write me a Postgres migration that adds a nullable `archived_at` column to invoices and backfills old cancelled rows.
I have a failing React test around a date picker and need help fixing the test code.
Can you benchmark this Python function and make it faster? I already have profiling output showing 80% of time is in one regex.
Generate a REST API client from this OpenAPI spec and save it under `src/generated`.
Summarize this design doc for leadership in five bullets. Don't change the architecture, just make it easier to read.
I already decided to split the auth service out. Can you scaffold the new FastAPI service and copy the existing endpoints into it?
Please review this PR for correctness. It renames a bunch of billing fields and updates serializers. I want code-review comments, not architecture advice.
Take this slow SQL query from production, explain the query plan, and suggest indexes. I don't need broad system design help, just database tuning.
I'm making a slide for the CTO comparing monoliths and microservices at a high level. Keep it neutral and presentation-friendly; don't turn it into a recommendation for my codebase.
